primaryMeaning: To deviate from the right course (go astray)
quranicRelevance: Straying/going astray from guidance (Ghayy - 'qad tabayyana ar-rushdu min al-ghayy'), and those who stray/deviate (Ghawoon - 'wash-shu'ara'u yattabi'uhumu al-ghawoon').

keyDerivations.meaning: To deviate, go astray, err, or collapse
Deviant ones, or those who go astray
To lead astray, delude, or deceive

figurativeOrRareUsages: مَا ضَلَّ صَاحِبُكُمْ وَمَا غَوَى (53:2): Your companion has not strayed, nor has he erred/deviated.
discardedNoise: Sectarian definitions of deviant movements in later history.
summaryEn: The root refers to deviation from guidance, depicting the path of error (Ghayy), leading astray (Ighwa'), and those who follow poets in vanity (Ghawoon).
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الانحراف عن الهدى، واصفاً مسالك الغي والضلال (غي)، والإغواء الشيطاني، والغاويين التابعين للشعراء في لغوهم.
rawText:
غوى 1 : see 4. ___ [And see also غَوِيثٌ.]
غوى 1 see what next follows.
